title: Horror crash death toll at 14
bodyText:
Breaking News
                            Update

NATIONAL NEWS- Transport and Community Safety MEC Xolile Nqatha has expressed deep sadness and sorrow over the horrific accident that claimed 14 lives when a SUV and a minibus taxi collided head-on on the N9 between Graaff-Reinet and Middelburg early Monday morning.


> The cause of the crash is still unknown and under investigation.


On behalf of the provincial government, Nqatha expressed his sincere condolences to the grieving families and wishes a speedy recovery to the injured passengers. MEC Nqatha continues to urge motorists to drive cautiously and adhere to the rules of the road.

Read more on the Graaff-Reinet Advertiser

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
